description: Cross-exchange comparison: Kraken vs MEXC for 4H DualConfirm hnotrl_msp20 strategy

kraken.fee_note: Kraken SPOT taker 26bps per side (standard tier)

mexc.fee_note: MEXC SPOT taker 10bps per side (0% maker, conservative taker-only)

head_to_head.description: Apples-to-apples: 360d proxy data, cohort A, same strategy (hnotrl_msp20), each exchange's own fees

delta.note: MEXC better on all metrics, but trade count drops from 76 to 23 (70% fewer trades)
MEXC slope-sized passes G1-G4 but fails G5 (robustness split). Best MEXC result overall.

fee_isolation.description: Same MEXC data, different fee levels. Isolates fee contribution from universe difference.

fee_delta.note: Fee difference costs $69.80 across 23 trades ($3.03/trade). Same trade signals, only fee changes.
Fee difference costs $40.36 ($1.75/trade). Smaller impact because slope-sizing reduces position sizes (avg_scale=0.513).

total_fee_vs_universe.fee_contribution_note: MEXC fee 10bps vs Kraken 26bps saves $40 on slope-sized ($70 on baseline)
total_fee_vs_universe.universe_contribution_note: Total head-to-head delta ($97.15) minus fee delta ($40.36). MEXC universe accounts for $57.
total_fee_vs_universe.trade_count_note: MEXC 144 coins produce only 23 trades vs Kraken 170 coins producing 76 trades. Fewer coins = fewer bad trades filtered out.

universe_comparison.overlap_note: MEXC has 144 vs Kraken 170 coins in cohort A. 15% fewer coins but 70% fewer trades — the MEXC universe triggers fewer DualConfirm entries because MEXC coins may have different volatility/RSI profiles.

slope_sizing_impact.description: Slope-as-sizing converts negative baseline into positive on BOTH exchanges
slope_sizing_impact.conclusion: Slope-sizing is ESSENTIAL on both exchanges. Turns -$669 -> +$62 on Kraken, -$58 -> +$159 on MEXC.

kraken_native_reference.description: Kraken native 120d for reference (best Kraken results, not directly comparable to 360d)
kraken_native_reference.note: 120d native Kraken is GO because window falls in steep downtrend regime. Not directly comparable to 360d extended.

reasons: Only 23 trades on 144 coins over 360 days (vs 76 on Kraken 170 coins)
Best MEXC config (slope-sized, MEXC fees) passes G1-G4 but fails G5 (robustness split: H1 -$114, H2 +$273)
MEXC baseline is negative (-$58) even with lower fees
Insufficient trade count for statistical confidence (23 is just barely above G1=15 threshold)
Trade concentration: 70% fewer trades than Kraken extended data — not enough market exposure
key_findings: Fee delta is modest: $40-70 across 23 trades ($1.75-3.03/trade)
Universe difference is the primary driver: MEXC coins trigger fewer DualConfirm entries
Slope-sizing is essential on BOTH exchanges: converts negative baseline to positive
The 4H DualConfirm strategy is structurally regime-dependent regardless of exchange
cross_exchange_observations: DualConfirm bounce needs specific volatility/RSI profiles that occur more on Kraken's coin universe
MEXC USDT pairs behave differently from Kraken USD pairs in mean-reversion characteristics
Lower fees alone do not rescue a strategy that lacks sufficient signal generation
Slope-sizing is a universal improvement pattern (works on both exchanges)
